title: Hattie's Block-Built Nature Walk Adventure
summary: When a rainy day thwarts Hattie the Mouse's plans for an outdoor nature walk, she turns to her Nana for comfort. With her mother away at the hospital tending to a new baby, Hattie and Nana transform their home into a vibrant landscape using only blocks. Their imaginary journey brings them face-to-face with squirrels, dandelions, and fantastical creatures like a striped horse in glasses and an elephant by the sea. This charming adventure proves that even the grayest day can be brightened with a little creativity and a handful of blocks.

highlightQuote.text: I saw an elephant with a flower on its head with the big stormy seas.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A little something to keep my flowers for when I have dirt.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A little something for what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Other home's dirt.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Jesus, Jesus...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There's the stars. Now it's time for stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Always about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Always about Hattie. Yeah. All right, there's our timer. You want to sit in my lap?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: In your lap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was very upset because she had a big problem. What was Hattie's problem today?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nothing. Just nothing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well what did Hattie want to do today?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie wanted to go for a nature but it was rain and she had to tell Mama about it but Mama was out with her new baby so she had to talk to Nana about it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Hattie Mouse said, "Nana, I just want to go on a nature walk. But it's raining so I can't go and I wanted to tell Mama about it but she was at the hospital with her new baby, what should I do?" And what did Nana say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can just play a little bit with blocks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: My blocks, my blocks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, let's play with blocks. And Nana said, "Great..."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, in the hall and in your bedroom, Nana Mouse, you have some blocks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, and Nana Mouse said let's play with blocks. I wonder what we could build. Maybe we could do a pretend nature walk with our blocks. Hmm, what might we see if we took a nature walk? We could build those things with blocks. Hmm, I'm imagining going on a nature walk and I'm walking along the grass and I see a tiny dandelion. I'll make that with my blocks. Okay, your turn Hattie, what do you see on the nature walk?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I see a bug which is called a worm.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a worm. Oh. Are you going to make your worm out of blocks?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Nana said, "Okay, now it's my turn." And I walk along and I hear a chirp-chirp-chirp-chirp and I look all around, up and down and side to side, but I can't see where that bird sound is coming from. Oh, it's up there in a tree. Oh, flap-flap-flap, it flew away. And I'll build a little birdie out of blocks. And maybe a bird's nest too. Okay your turn Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I saw a bug and some creepy crawlies and some slugs and bugs and some critters.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Slugs and bugs and creepy crawlies and critters, that's awesome. Those are really good things to see on a nature walk. Um, and Nana said, and then I saw a little gray fuzzy shape running really fast straight up a tree with a big bushy tail. Do you know what it was?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a squirrel.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, it was a squirrel that I saw and it was looking for some nuts. Okay your turn Hattie, what did you see next on the nature walk?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, let's imagine what I saw on the nature walk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, let's imagine.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I saw a... I saw a big pink tail on an elephant who saw... who saw me.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I saw an elephant with a flower on its head with the big stormy seas.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, an elephant with a flower on its head and big stormy seas. Oh. Well then I saw... um... a horse with pink and blue stripes and big funny glasses.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Haha. Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What did you see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I saw a flower and a funny little flower and big spider trying to get the nectar, but it was raining, so an ant came inside and a worm too. That's what I saw.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that's a very interesting thing to see on a nature walk. I saw a big fat woodchuck. And it went waddle-waddle-waddle and it said, "Excuse me, do you have any vegetables? I want to eat them." And we said, "I'm sorry, no, you already ate all the vegetables in our garden." And he said, "Oh, too bad, I'll go to the garden next door." What did you see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Maybe that was the end of the nature walk?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe you can build with blocks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, we were building our nature walk with blocks. And so Nana and Hattie the mouse built a beautiful nature walk with blocks...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: ...and some foxes and ants and squirrels and elephants and bunnies and foxes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, there were so many different kinds of creatures on their nature walk. And then they did a pretend nature walk by walking with their fingers just like Sneaky Mouse, walk-walk-walk-walk-walk, through their blocks. And they saw all the different creatures and all the different plants and all the different creepy crawlies and slugs and bugs and butterflies and worms and insects and all kinds of things. And then they came back home because they were done with their nature walk. And so when they were done, they put away all their blocks and they said, oh Nana said, "Thank you for such a nice pretend nature walk with blocks." And Hattie said, "Oh thank you Nana, I thought it was a great pretend adventure that we had." And that's the end of Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, put all the flowers back where they lived.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, that's a good idea. Putting the flowers back. I like that idea. One of them's in the chair there. Here, I'll turn off the light.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, you put the flowers back where they lived.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, that's always a good idea.

characters.name: Hattie
characters.description: A clever mouse milliner and engineer of whimsy, she is a resourceful spirit who crafts exquisite hats and soulful portraits within her hollow tree stump workshop. Though a formidable presence famously counted among the thirty legendary cats, she is currently confined to her bed by illness, her usual treks through saturated woodlands for rare tape and lollipops temporarily halted. Even in her malaise, her sharp mind lingers on a recent, stinging disagreement with Dimitar over a fish, a rare shadow cast upon a heart otherwise known for its boundless generosity and creative light.

characters.name: Nana
characters.description: The dignified mole matriarch and grandmother to Hattie, Pistis, and Isabella, this pillar of watchful wisdom resides at the end of the train line, tackling life’s problems "one at a time." From engineering mouse-scale Lego structures to the meticulous restoration of dollhouses using patient glue and tiny pencils, her steady paws bridge the gap between architectural feats and delicate artistry. Whether discerning potential as a trolley passenger or welcoming weary travelers into her sanctuary, her quiet authority is so legendary that she graces the cover of Hattie’s book in a rare, peaceful slumber. Even in rest, she remains the heart of their world—a steady warmth anchoring every journey’s end, her sharp eyes softening for Izzy’s mushroom-textured crafts.

characters.name: Mama
characters.description: Pistis Mouse’s mother, the architect of the naming that defined her daughter, is a maternal mole who slips away to the store by day and is currently away at the hospital. She remains the quiet destination at the journey’s end: a presence anchored to an upstairs chair, waiting to beckon her kitten into the velvet warmth of her lap for a soft, lingering embrace.

characters.name: New Baby
characters.description: Hattie's new sibling at the hospital.

characters.name: Worm
characters.description: A bug seen during the imaginary nature walk.

characters.name: Bird
characters.description: High within the foliage, the creature’s rhythmic chirping signals a steady recovery from a sprained wing that once required clinical X-rays and a precise regimen of specialized pain medicine.

characters.name: Squirrel
characters.description: A fuzzy gray creature with a voluminous, silver tail, once only a phantom heard crunching on neighborhood acorns, now reveals itself as it forages for nuts with jittery grace.

characters.name: Elephant
characters.description: A colossal, trumpeting behemoth crowned with a vibrant blossom, haunting the misty threshold where salt-sprayed jungles meet turbulent, stormy seas. It remains a stoic, floral-crested sentinel, unmoved as the high-octane shriek of race cars blurs through its ancient, verdant domain.

characters.name: Horse
characters.description: A creature with pink and blue stripes wearing funny glasses.

characters.name: Woodchuck
characters.description: A fat creature looking for vegetables to eat.

characters.name: Sneaky Mouse
characters.description: A mouse persona used for walking through the block landscape with fingers.

characters.name: Spider
characters.description: A bug trying to get nectar despite the rain.

characters.name: Ant
characters.description: A small insect that decides to come inside.

places.name: The Hospital
places.description: The clinical hub for surgeries and examinations, now transformed into a hushed, antiseptic sanctuary where Mama and her newborn recover amidst the sterile hum of life-sustaining machinery.

places.name: Nana's Hall
places.description: A location where Nana Mouse keeps some of her blocks.

places.name: Nana's Bedroom
places.description: Another room where Hattie and Nana find blocks for their adventure.

places.name: The Stormy Seas
places.description: An imaginary location where the elephant with a flower lives.

places.name: The Garden
places.description: Behind the house, a junk-strewn expanse gives way to the tangled thicket, a strategic blind overlooking the decimated vegetable patch. Once used to ambush the pool noodle nibbler, it now guards the hollowed stalks of a woodchuck’s feast, its floor a messy tapestry of discarded refuse and rabbit droppings.

places.name: The Garden Next Door
places.description: Where the hungry woodchuck heads to find more food.

places.name: Hattie's Home
places.description: The final destination where Hattie and Nana return after their nature walk.
